free_text: Scattering of alpha particles by oxygen. I. Bombarding
energy range 5.8 to 10.0 MeV
Oxygen target prepared by vacuum deposition of thin
layers of lithium metal onto thin carbon backings
with subsequent oxidation of the lithium to form LiOH.
Targets 25-35 keV thick to 10-Me alpha-particles.
For normalization: The SiO2 targets were prepared from
blown quartz, and their thicknesses were determined
by weighing.
p-type silicon-junction detectors.
Because of uncertainties in determination of the exact
oxygen content of the LiOH targets, the absolute cross
sections were assigned by normalizing the data to cross
sections measured with Si02 targets whose thicknesses
were known to within 6%. Exclusive use of quartz
targets was precluded by the frequent overlap of
silicon inelastic and oxygen elastic groups.
90 degree analyzing magnet calibrated using well-known
13C(p,n)13N threshold with assumed threshold of 3.237
MeV.
Additional checks on the calibration and linearity of
the magnet were provided using (p,n) threshold
measurements and by threshold measurements of the
6Li(a,n)9B and 7Li(a,n)10B reactions. The 7Li(a,n)10B
threshold at 4.37 MeV (lab) was determined with both
singly and doubly charged helium beams.
Maximum calibration error not more than 20 keV, and
probably not more than 10 keV.
The estimated error in the cross sections
is 12% (from normalizations to data from SiO2 target),
except for the region of excitation below 6.3 MeV,
where statistical errors of +-10% resulted from very
poor beam currents (the order of 1 nA). Statistical
errors also increased the cross-section errors near
the minimum of the angular distribution where the
cross section is less than 10 mb/sr.
SD. Duplication in energy(or angle) values
in Subents 2,3 were checked and corrected. BIB upgaded
Cosmetic changes in all Subents.
Digitizing error in energy 0.02 MeV.
Digitizing error in data 1 mb/sr.
Data scanned from Fig.2-3 of PR,160,782,1967
at CNPD.
SD. Duplication in energy values corrected.
Digitizing error in angle 1 degree.
Digitizing error in data 1%.
Data scanned from Fig.4-6 in reference at NPDC.
SD. Duplication in angle values corrected.
Resonance assignment uncertain.
Spin and parity assignment uncertain.
Resonance assignment uncertain. Spin either
2 or 6.
Spin parity either 0+, 1-, or 3-.
Data taken from Table 1 of PR,160,782,1967.
